{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Senior Internal Auditor",
  "description": "dLocal is the #1 payments leader focused on emerging markets and helps some of the best companies in the world expand in emerging countries. Global brands such as Amazon and Google rely on us to increase conversion by eliminating complexity and managing their payments expansion effortlessly. As both a payments processor and a merchant of record where we operate, we make it simple and risk-free for our client partners to make inroads into the world&rsquo;s fastest-growing, emerging markets.

We are&nbsp;recruiting to fill the position below:

&nbsp;Job Position: Senior Internal Auditor

Job Location: Lagos
Job type: Full-time
Work mode: Hybrid

Job Description


	We are looking for a highly analytical and detail-oriented Senior Internal Auditor to join our team in Nigeria.
	In this role, you will be part of our global Internal Audit function and will participate in several audit assignments by identifying risks, evaluating internal controls, assessing compliance with internal policies and external regulations, and driving continuous improvement across the organization.
	You will work closely with cross-functional teams and contribute to strengthening governance and operational excellence.


What will I be doing?


	Execute and report risk?based internal audits over business processes, IT domains and regulatory matters.
	Perform design and operating effectiveness testing, including walkthroughs, control testing and evaluation of deficiencies.
	Maintain awareness of changing regulatory requirements (e.g. CBN), audit standards, and best practices.
	Assess the impact of new products, systems, process changes and reorganizations on the control environment, providing independent challenge and recommendations.
	Prepare clear, structured audit reports that highlight key risks, root causes and practical, risk?based recommendations for management.
	Follow up on agreed action plans, monitoring remediation of control deficiencies and supporting management in defining sustainable solutions.
	Build strong and trusted relationships with several stakeholders across divisions, positioning Internal Audit as a partner in internal control matters.
	Contribute to the evolution of our audit methodology through automation, continuous auditing and the use of AI/advanced analytics where applicable.Conduct internal audits across IT and business process areas.


What skills do I need?


	4 - 5 years of professional experience in Internal Audit / Internal Controls or related fields.
	Experience in fintech, financial services, or regulated industries
	Business Process, Compliance/AML &amp; IT General Controls knowledge.
	SOX experience is desirable.
	Knowledge of internal control frameworks, audit methodologies, and risk assessment practices.
	Ability to work independently and collaboratively in a global environment.
	High integrity, confidentiality, and strong ethical standards.
	Strong stakeholder management and communication skills.
	Advanced English proficiency (written and spoken).
	Excellent written and verbal communication skills.
	High attention to detail with a proactive and structured approach.
	Strong problem-solving skills and ability to handle multiple priorities.


Bonus points


	Professional certifications or progress toward certifications (CISA, CIA, etc.).
	Additional languages.
",
  "datePosted": "2026-07-23",
  "validThrough": "2026-08-24",
  "employmentType": "Full-Time",

    "name": "dLocal"
    
  },

    }

}



Senior Internal Auditor Job at dLocal




        
    

        
        dLocal is the #1 payments leader focused on emerging markets and helps some of the best companies in the world expand in emerging countries. Global brands such as Amazon and Google rely on us to increase conversion by eliminating complexity and managing their payments expansion effortlessly. As both a payments processor and a merchant of record where we operate, we make it simple and risk-free for our client partners to make inroads into the world’s fastest-growing, emerging markets.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

 Job Position: Senior Internal Auditor

Job Location: Lagos
Job type: Full-time
Work mode: Hybrid

Job Description
     

            
    




	We are looking for a highly analytical and detail-oriented Senior Internal Auditor to join our team in Nigeria.
	In this role, you will be part of our global Internal Audit function and will participate in several audit assignments by identifying risks, evaluating internal controls, assessing compliance with internal policies and external regulations, and driving continuous improvement across the organization.
	You will work closely with cross-functional teams and contribute to strengthening governance and operational excellence.


What will I be doing?


	Execute and report risk?based internal audits over business processes, IT domains and regulatory matters.
	Perform design and operating effectiveness testing, including walkthroughs, control testing and evaluation of deficiencies.
	Maintain awareness of changing regulatory requirements (e.g. CBN), audit standards, and best practices.
	Assess the impact of new products, systems, process changes and reorganizations on the control environment, providing independent challenge and recommendations.
	Prepare clear, structured audit reports that highlight key risks, root causes and practical, risk?based recommendations for management.
	Follow up on agreed action plans, monitoring remediation of control deficiencies and supporting management in defining sustainable solutions.
	Build strong and trusted relationships with several stakeholders across divisions, positioning Internal Audit as a partner in internal control matters.
	Contribute to the evolution of our audit methodology through automation, continuous auditing and the use of AI/advanced analytics where applicable.Conduct internal audits across IT and business process areas.


What skills do I need?


	4 - 5 years of professional experience in Internal Audit / Internal Controls or related fields.
	Experience in fintech, financial services, or regulated industries
	Business Process, Compliance/AML & IT General Controls knowledge.
	SOX experience is desirable.
	Knowledge of internal control frameworks, audit methodologies, and risk assessment practices.
	Ability to work independently and collaboratively in a global environment.
	High integrity, confidentiality, and strong ethical standards.
	Strong stakeholder management and communication skills.
	Advanced English proficiency (written and spoken).
	Excellent written and verbal communication skills.
	High attention to detail with a proactive and structured approach.
	Strong problem-solving skills and ability to handle multiple priorities.


Bonus points


	Professional certifications or progress toward certifications (CISA, CIA, etc.).
	Additional languages.
    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Junior Accountant",
  "description": "Visco Group of Companies Limited - A trusted name in culinary, healthcare, hospitality, and essential services. We are more than a business; we are an ecosystem designed to enrich everyday living. From vibrant restaurants &amp; lounges that redefine dining, to premium homes &amp; apartments that set new standards in comfort, from pharmacies &amp; supermarkets delivering health and convenience, to advanced laboratory &amp; diagnostic centers ensuring reliable healthcare solutions. We bring innovation, quality, and care into every service we offer. At Visco Group, our mission is simple: to deliver excellence, create value, and improve lives.

We are&nbsp;&nbsp;recruiting to fill the position below:

&nbsp;Job Position: Junior Accountant

Job Location: Benin, Edo
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	We are seeking a detail-oriented and organized Junior Accountant with a strong focus on inventory management to support accurate stock control and financial record-keeping across our operations.
	The Junior Accountant will be responsible for tracking inventory movements, reconciling stock records with financial data, supporting audits, and ensuring proper documentation of inventory-related transactions.
	The ideal candidate has a solid accounting foundation, strong analytical skills, and a high level of integrity.


Key Responsibilities


	Maintain accurate inventory records across all business units.
	Track inventory inflow and outflow, including purchases, transfers, sales, and returns.
	Reconcile physical stock counts with system records and investigate variances.
	Support periodic stock counts, spot checks, and year-end inventory audits.
	Record inventory-related transactions in accounting systems and spreadsheets.
	Monitor stock valuation, cost of goods sold (COGS), and expiry-related losses.
	Ensure proper documentation for inventory movements and approvals.
	Assist in preparing inventory and financial reports for management review.
	Support month-end and year-end closing activities related to inventory accounts.
	Flag slow-moving, expired, or obsolete stock and recommend corrective actions.
	Ensure compliance with internal controls and company policies.


Requirements


	HND or Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree in Accounting, Finance, or a related field.
	1&ndash;2 years of experience in accounting or inventory-related roles would be an advantage.
	Basic knowledge of accounting principles and inventory management.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Excel and accounting software (Zoho Books, QuickBooks, or similar is an advantage).
	Strong attention to detail and numerical accuracy.
	Good analytical, organizational, and reporting skills.
	Ability to work independently and collaboratively.
	High level of integrity and confidentiality.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CVs to hr@viscoglobalgroup.com using the Job Position as the subject of the email",

Junior Accountant Job at Visco Group of Companies Limited




        
    

        
        Visco Group of Companies Limited - A trusted name in culinary, healthcare, hospitality, and essential services. We are more than a business; we are an ecosystem designed to enrich everyday living. From vibrant restaurants & lounges that redefine dining, to premium homes & apartments that set new standards in comfort, from pharmacies & supermarkets delivering health and convenience, to advanced laboratory & diagnostic centers ensuring reliable healthcare solutions. We bring innovation, quality, and care into every service we offer. At Visco Group, our mission is simple: to deliver excellence, create value, and improve lives.

We are  recruiting to fill the position below:

 Job Position: Junior Accountant
     

            
    



Job Location: Benin, Edo
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	We are seeking a detail-oriented and organized Junior Accountant with a strong focus on inventory management to support accurate stock control and financial record-keeping across our operations.
	The Junior Accountant will be responsible for tracking inventory movements, reconciling stock records with financial data, supporting audits, and ensuring proper documentation of inventory-related transactions.
	The ideal candidate has a solid accounting foundation, strong analytical skills, and a high level of integrity.


Key Responsibilities


	Maintain accurate inventory records across all business units.
	Track inventory inflow and outflow, including purchases, transfers, sales, and returns.
	Reconcile physical stock counts with system records and investigate variances.
	Support periodic stock counts, spot checks, and year-end inventory audits.
	Record inventory-related transactions in accounting systems and spreadsheets.
	Monitor stock valuation, cost of goods sold (COGS), and expiry-related losses.
	Ensure proper documentation for inventory movements and approvals.
	Assist in preparing inventory and financial reports for management review.
	Support month-end and year-end closing activities related to inventory accounts.
	Flag slow-moving, expired, or obsolete stock and recommend corrective actions.
	Ensure compliance with internal controls and company policies.


Requirements


	HND or Bachelor’s Degree in Accounting, Finance, or a related field.
	1–2 years of experience in accounting or inventory-related roles would be an advantage.
	Basic knowledge of accounting principles and inventory management.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Excel and accounting software (Zoho Books, QuickBooks, or similar is an advantage).
	Strong attention to detail and numerical accuracy.
	Good analytical, organizational, and reporting skills.
	Ability to work independently and collaboratively.
	High level of integrity and confidentiality.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CVs to [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Human Resources Manager",
  "description": "Visco Group of Companies Limited - A trusted name in culinary, healthcare, hospitality, and essential services. We are more than a business; we are an ecosystem designed to enrich everyday living. From vibrant restaurants &amp; lounges that redefine dining, to premium homes &amp; apartments that set new standards in comfort, from pharmacies &amp; supermarkets delivering health and convenience, to advanced laboratory &amp; diagnostic centers ensuring reliable healthcare solutions. We bring innovation, quality, and care into every service we offer. At Visco Group, our mission is simple: to deliver excellence, create value, and improve lives.

We are&nbsp;recruiting to fill the position below:

&nbsp;Job Position: Human Resources Manager

Job Location: Benin, Edo
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	We are seeking a highly organized, proactive, and experienced Human Resources Manager to oversee all HR operations across Visco Group of Companies Limited.
	The ideal candidate will be responsible for&nbsp;recruitment, employee relations, performance management, HR compliance, training, and overall workforce planning.
	This role requires strong leadership, excellent communication skills, sound judgment, and the ability to align HR strategies with organizational goals.


Responsibilities
Recruitment &amp; Talent Acquisition:


	Lead and manage end-to-end recruitment processes for all company vacancies.
	Develop strategies to attract, assess, and retain qualified candidates.
	Oversee onboarding and orientation of new employees.


HR Administration &amp; Policy Management:


	Maintain accurate and up-to-date employee records and HR documentation.
	Develop, update, and enforce HR policies, employee handbooks, and SOPs.
	Ensure full compliance with Nigerian labour laws and industry regulations.


Performance Management:


	Implement and coordinate performance appraisal systems.
	Support department heads in setting KPIs and tracking employee performance.
	Recommend performance improvement plans where necessary.


Employee Relations:


	Resolve workplace conflicts in a fair and professional manner.
	Promote a positive work environment and strong employee engagement.
	Conduct investigations on disciplinary matters and issue queries when needed.


Payroll &amp; Compensation Support:


	Collaborate with the Accounts department to ensure accurate payroll processing.
	Maintain confidentiality of employee compensation and HR-related information.


Training &amp; Development:


	Identify staff training needs and facilitate meaningful learning programs.
	Support continuous professional development initiatives.


Compliance, Health &amp; Safety:


	Ensure compliance with labour standards, company regulations, and safety policies.
	Maintain HR risk assessments and support health and safety initiatives.


Reporting &amp; Management Support:


	Prepare and present HR reports to management as required.
	Advise leadership on HR strategies, workforce planning, and organizational improvement.


Requirements


	Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree in Human Resources Management, Business Administration, Industrial Relations, or related field.
	Minimum of 2 years of experience in HR management or a senior HR role.
	Strong knowledge of Nigerian labour laws and HR best practices.
	Excellent leadership, communication, and interpersonal skills.
	Strong problem-solving, conflict resolution, and decision-making abilities.
	Proficiency in HR software and Microsoft Office applications.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their Applications to: hr@viscoglobalgroup.com using the Job Position as the subject of the email",

Human Resources Manager Job at Visco Group of Companies Limited




        
    

        
        Visco Group of Companies Limited - A trusted name in culinary, healthcare, hospitality, and essential services. We are more than a business; we are an ecosystem designed to enrich everyday living. From vibrant restaurants & lounges that redefine dining, to premium homes & apartments that set new standards in comfort, from pharmacies & supermarkets delivering health and convenience, to advanced laboratory & diagnostic centers ensuring reliable healthcare solutions. We bring innovation, quality, and care into every service we offer. At Visco Group, our mission is simple: to deliver excellence, create value, and improve lives.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

 Job Position: Human Resources Manager
     

            
    



Job Location: Benin, Edo
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	We are seeking a highly organized, proactive, and experienced Human Resources Manager to oversee all HR operations across Visco Group of Companies Limited.
	The ideal candidate will be responsible for recruitment, employee relations, performance management, HR compliance, training, and overall workforce planning.
	This role requires strong leadership, excellent communication skills, sound judgment, and the ability to align HR strategies with organizational goals.


Responsibilities
Recruitment & Talent Acquisition:


	Lead and manage end-to-end recruitment processes for all company vacancies.
	Develop strategies to attract, assess, and retain qualified candidates.
	Oversee onboarding and orientation of new employees.


HR Administration & Policy Management:


	Maintain accurate and up-to-date employee records and HR documentation.
	Develop, update, and enforce HR policies, employee handbooks, and SOPs.
	Ensure full compliance with Nigerian labour laws and industry regulations.


Performance Management:


	Implement and coordinate performance appraisal systems.
	Support department heads in setting KPIs and tracking employee performance.
	Recommend performance improvement plans where necessary.


Employee Relations:


	Resolve workplace conflicts in a fair and professional manner.
	Promote a positive work environment and strong employee engagement.
	Conduct investigations on disciplinary matters and issue queries when needed.


Payroll & Compensation Support:


	Collaborate with the Accounts department to ensure accurate payroll processing.
	Maintain confidentiality of employee compensation and HR-related information.


Training & Development:


	Identify staff training needs and facilitate meaningful learning programs.
	Support continuous professional development initiatives.


Compliance, Health & Safety:


	Ensure compliance with labour standards, company regulations, and safety policies.
	Maintain HR risk assessments and support health and safety initiatives.


Reporting & Management Support:


	Prepare and present HR reports to management as required.
	Advise leadership on HR strategies, workforce planning, and organizational improvement.


Requirements


	Bachelor’s Degree in Human Resources Management, Business Administration, Industrial Relations, or related field.
	Minimum of 2 years of experience in HR management or a senior HR role.
	Strong knowledge of Nigerian labour laws and HR best practices.
	Excellent leadership, communication, and interpersonal skills.
	Strong problem-solving, conflict resolution, and decision-making abilities.
	Proficiency in HR software and Microsoft Office applications.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their Applications to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Inventory Officer",
  "description": "Workland is a career-focused platform dedicated to helping individuals and organizations connect through meaningful work, skills, and opportunities.

We operate at the intersection of employment, career development, and skills monetization, providing job seekers with practical guidance while supporting employers with access to informed, prepared, and opportunity-ready talent.

We are&nbsp;recruiting to fill the position below:

&nbsp;Job Position: Inventory Officer

Job Location: Lagos

Job Summary


	We are seeking a detail-oriented and organized Inventory Officer to manage the company&#39;s inventory, ensure accurate stock records, and support efficient warehouse operations.
	The successful candidate will be responsible for receiving, storing, tracking, and issuing inventory while maintaining optimal stock levels and minimizing losses.


Key Responsibilities


	Receive, inspect, and record all incoming inventory and supplies.
	Maintain accurate inventory records using manual and/or digital inventory systems.
	Monitor stock levels and notify management when inventory reaches reorder levels.
	Conduct routine stock counts and periodic physical inventory audits.
	Investigate and reconcile inventory discrepancies.
	Ensure proper storage and organization of inventory to prevent damage or loss.
	Issue materials and products according to approved requests.
	Prepare inventory reports, stock movement reports, and monthly inventory summaries.
	&nbsp;Work closely with procurement, warehouse, and finance teams to ensure inventory accuracy.
	Ensure compliance with company policies and inventory control procedures.
	Maintain a clean, safe, and organized store or warehouse environment.
	Assist in implementing inventory improvement initiatives and cost-saving measures.


Requirements


	Minimum of an OND, HND, or Bachelor&#39;s Degree in Business Administration, Logistics, Supply Chain Management, Accounting, or a related field.
	1 - 3 years of experience in inventory management, warehouse operations, or a similar role.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Excel and inventory management software is an added advantage.
	Strong numerical, analytical, and record-keeping skills.
	Excellent organizational and time management abilities.
	High level of integrity, accountability, and attention to detail.
	Good communication and interpersonal skills.
	Ability to work independently and as part of a team.


Preferred Skills:


	Experience using ERP or inventory management systems.
	Knowledge of inventory control principles and warehouse best practices.
	Ability to prepare reports and analyze stock trends.
	Problem-solving and decision-making skills.


What We Offer


	Competitive salary of N150,000 and above (commensurate with experience).
	Opportunities for professional growth and career advancement.
	Supportive and collaborative work environment.
	Training and development opportunities.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV and a brief cover letter to: worklandhq@gmail.com using the Job Position as the subject of the email.

Note: Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted.",

Inventory Officer Job at Workland




        
    

        
        Workland is a career-focused platform dedicated to helping individuals and organizations connect through meaningful work, skills, and opportunities.

We operate at the intersection of employment, career development, and skills monetization, providing job seekers with practical guidance while supporting employers with access to informed, prepared, and opportunity-ready talent.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

 Job Position: Inventory Officer

Job Location: Lagos

Job Summary
     

            
    




	We are seeking a detail-oriented and organized Inventory Officer to manage the company's inventory, ensure accurate stock records, and support efficient warehouse operations.
	The successful candidate will be responsible for receiving, storing, tracking, and issuing inventory while maintaining optimal stock levels and minimizing losses.


Key Responsibilities


	Receive, inspect, and record all incoming inventory and supplies.
	Maintain accurate inventory records using manual and/or digital inventory systems.
	Monitor stock levels and notify management when inventory reaches reorder levels.
	Conduct routine stock counts and periodic physical inventory audits.
	Investigate and reconcile inventory discrepancies.
	Ensure proper storage and organization of inventory to prevent damage or loss.
	Issue materials and products according to approved requests.
	Prepare inventory reports, stock movement reports, and monthly inventory summaries.
	 Work closely with procurement, warehouse, and finance teams to ensure inventory accuracy.
	Ensure compliance with company policies and inventory control procedures.
	Maintain a clean, safe, and organized store or warehouse environment.
	Assist in implementing inventory improvement initiatives and cost-saving measures.


Requirements


	Minimum of an OND, HND, or Bachelor's Degree in Business Administration, Logistics, Supply Chain Management, Accounting, or a related field.
	1 - 3 years of experience in inventory management, warehouse operations, or a similar role.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Excel and inventory management software is an added advantage.
	Strong numerical, analytical, and record-keeping skills.
	Excellent organizational and time management abilities.
	High level of integrity, accountability, and attention to detail.
	Good communication and interpersonal skills.
	Ability to work independently and as part of a team.


Preferred Skills:


	Experience using ERP or inventory management systems.
	Knowledge of inventory control principles and warehouse best practices.
	Ability to prepare reports and analyze stock trends.
	Problem-solving and decision-making skills.


What We Offer


	Competitive salary of N150,000 and above (commensurate with experience).
	Opportunities for professional growth and career advancement.
	Supportive and collaborative work environment.
	Training and development opportunities.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV and a brief cover letter to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.

Note: Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted.    

    

        Click here to Apply

Tax Accounting Specialist Job at Montego Upstream Services Limited




        
    

        
        Montego primarily refers to Montego Holdings, a prominent energy and natural resources group headquartered in Ikoyi, Lagos, or alternatively to Montego Pet Nutrition and the antibiotic medication Montego 100mg.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Tax Accounting Specialist

Job Location: Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Overview
     

            
    




	The successful candidate will ensure timely and accurate preparation and filing of all tax returns (CIT, VAT, WHT, etc.).
	The ideal candidate will have a strong understanding of Nigerian tax regulations and hands-on experience working with tax authorities.


Qualifications


	Bachelor’s degree in Accounting, Finance, or relevant field.
	Minimum of 5 years of progressive experience in similar job role.
	Must be a Chartered Accountant (ICAN/ACCA).
	Strong knowledge of Nigerian tax laws and regulations.
	Practical experience dealing with FIRS, NIRS and State IRS (e.g., LIRS).
	Proven experience in Transfer Pricing.
	Proficiency in SAP and Microsoft Excel.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply
